data.title: Medical Properties tenant Prospect Medical Files for bankruptcy protection: report
data.description:
Prospect Medical Holdings filed for bankruptcy protection on Saturday, citing struggles with debt and soaring expenses, Bloomberg reported Sunday, citing court documents filed in Texas.

The privately held tenant of Medical Properties Trust (NYSE:MPW) listed between $1B and $10B in both assets and liabilities, according to the report.

Prospect Medical, once an active buyer of financially troubled hospitals, provides services in Southern California, Connecticut, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, and Rhode Island, its website says.

The company also manages networks of more than 11K doctors and operates 16 hospitals, according to its website.

Medical Properties last year tried to take over some of Prospect’s health care operations, saying it defaulted in debt.

Medical Properties acquired 16 hospitals operated by Prospect in 2019. He owned 402 facilities and around 40K beds in nine countries across three continents at the end of September.

In November, Medical Properties announced a binding agreement for the sale of a majority of Prospect’s managed care platform to Astrana Health (ASTH) for around $745M and the assumption of certain liabilities. The deal is expected to close in the middle of this year.

Neither Prospect Medical nor Medical Properties immeidately responded to requests for comment by Seeking Alpha.

data.title: 10 levered big-caps that may face higher refinancing expenses
data.description:
Bond yields have been rising as improved economic data quell investor hopes that the Federal Reserve and other central banks will cut borrowing costs to stimulate growth. A monthly jobs report on Friday that was much better than estimated drove yields on 10-year Treasurys up to 4.772%, the highest close in two months.

A persistent rise in the cost to borrow may mean large-cap companies that carry significant debt will face higher rates when they refinance their debt. The following stock screen shows companies with a debt-to-equity ratio greater than 200%.

Applovin (NASDAQ:APP), Home Depot (NYSE:HD), Bristol-Myers Squibb (BMY) and Goldman Sachs Group (NYSE:GS) have the highest quant rating among the 10 large-cap stocks on the screen.

data.title: Trump favors lifting cap on deduction for state and local taxes, NY Republicans say
data.description:
President-elect Donald Trump said Saturday he favors raising the limit on deductions for state and local tax (SALT) deductions, according to House Republicans from New York.

Trump met with New York’s House delegation Saturday night at his Mar-a-Lago resort in Florida.

The 2017 tax-cut package included a $10K cap on SALT paid, which previously had no limit. Trump advisers since the 2024 election have floated a plan to double the deduction cap to $20K.

Trump ”reiterated his support for lifting the cap on SALT and ending congestion pricing. We will get it done,” Rep. Mike Lawler (R-N.Y.) said in a post on X.

Lawler reportedly told the New York Post that Trump talked to the lawmakers about “the need to come up with a number and work through it and build consensus in the House.”

Rep. Nicole Malliotakis (R-N.Y.) called the meeting “productive” in a post on X.

“He said that he understands the plight of New Yorkers who are being abused by our mayor and our governor who treat them like ATMs, and he wants to provide SALT relief,” Malliotakis was quoted as saying by the Post. “We need to work out what that number is going to be.”

Trump also reportedly promised to help try to end New York City’s recently imposed congestion program.

The Trump transition team did not immediately respond to a request for comment by Seeking Alpha.

More on US Politics

  • Trump plans meeting with SALT-focused Republicans - report

  • Trump announces Damac to make $20B investment in U.S. data centers

  • Yellen says U.S. debt limit to be reached in mid-January

  • Senate passes bill to prevent government shutdown, Biden signs it into law

data.title: Amid a global selloff, the right move might be to buy more bonds
data.description:
This week's headlines were dominated by economic data that led to a recalibration of Federal Reserve interest rate cuts and a major selloff in bonds.

Even artificial intelligence news from the likes of Nvidia (NVDA) and AMD (AMD) found itself relegated to the sidelines as labor market and inflation indicators that an easing Fed does not want to see took center stage.

U.S. Treasury yields surged on Friday as investors sold off bonds after the latest jobs report came in hotter than expected and further supported a case for fewer Federal Reserve interest rate cuts.

Following the strong economic data recently and remarks from central bank speakers, the outlook for rate cuts has significantly changed. Traders now see the first cut this year in September.

Another major reason behind the rise in U.S. Treasury yields has also been the uncertainty and concerns over the policies of U.S. President-elect Donald Trump. Trump's promised tariffs might prove to be inflationary, and his promised tax cuts could further increase budget deficits.

The benchmark 10-year yield (US10Y) rose as much as 10 basis points to 4.79% on Friday, approaching levels not seen since the end of October 2023. The 30-year yield (US30Y) hit 5.00%, while the shorter-end 2-year yield (US2Y) was up about 7 basis points.

The S&P 500 (SP500) on Friday slipped 1.94% for the holiday-shortened week to end at 5,827.04 points, posting losses in two out of four sessions. The S&P 500 (SP500) Technology sector was -3.10% for the week, with the Magnificent 7 club down -1.7%. Nvidia (NVDA) shed -5.9%.

Worries over fiscal discipline are also not restricted to the U.S., with borrowing costs in France now above those of Greece, while gilt yields in the U.K. have been pushed to their highest levels since 2008.

Still the safe haven?

Investors are demanding larger premiums in response to the uncertainty. However, according to a report from the Wall Street Journal, bonds might prove to the safest place to invest amid the turmoil.

The Journal calls today's situation, where central banks have been able to increase interest rates without harming the economy, and then slowly slash them while sending hawkish messages, unprecedented.

The report attributes the stock market's negative reaction on Friday to stretched valuations. After years of technology-led rallies, the S&P 500's one-year forward earnings yield has fallen to 4.6%, equal to the yield of a 5-year Treasury, thus making bonds increasingly competitive with equities.

And if there was truly a threat to growth and corporate earnings, central banks would go into stimulus mode, which would end up benefiting bonds, the report added.

Here are the best Quant Rated government bond ETFs:

  • Bondbloxx Bloomberg One Year Target Duration US Treasury ETF (XONE) - Quant Rating: 3.13
  • US Treasury 12 Month Bill ETF (OBIL) - Quant Rating: 3.13
  • Bondbloxx Bloomberg Two Year Target Duration US Treasury ETF (XTWO) - Quant Rating: 2.98
  • Jpmorgan Betabuilders U.S. Treasury Bond 1-3 Year ETF (BBSB) - Quant Rating: 2.97
  • Vanguard Short-Term Treasury Index Fund ETF Shares (VGSH) - Quant Rating: 2.96
  • SPDR® Portfolio Short Term Treasury ETF (SPTS) - Quant Rating: 2.95
  • US Treasury 2 Year Note ETF (UTWO) - Quant Rating: 2.93
  • iShares 1-3 Year Treasury Bond ETF (SHY) - Quant Rating: 2.90
  • Schwab Short-Term U.S. Treasury ETF™ (SCHO) - Quant Rating: 2.90
  • Franklin Short Duration U.S. Government ETF (FTSD) - Quant Rating: 2.87

data.title: Apple's board recommends shareholders vote against proposal to end diversity programs
data.description:
Apple’s board has recommended that its investors vote against a shareholder proposal to end the tech giant’s Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) programs, according to a proxy filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Conservative think-tank National Center Center for Public Policy had submitted a proposal for the iPhone maker to consider abolishing its “Inclusion & Diversity programs, policies, department and goals,” the filing said.

The proposal pointed to recent U.S. Supreme Court decisions, arguing that DEI programs pose “litigation, reputational and financial risks to companies” and make Apple more exposed to lawsuits over alleged discrimination.

“Apple is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate in recruiting, hiring, training, or promoting on any basis protected by law,” the company said in the filing.

A number of large companies, including Meta Platforms (META) and Amazon.com (AMZN), have announced they are ending DEI programs ahead of Republican President-elect Donald Trump’s return to the White House next week.

Conservative groups have voiced opposition to DEI programs and said they would sue over them following a 2023 Supreme Court ruling that struck down affirmative action in university admissions.

data.title: LA wildfires may be costliest in U.S. history, forecast says
data.description:
The death toll from the wildfires that have razed parts of Los Angeles rose to 16 by Sunday morning, and the disaster is estimated to be one of the costliest in U.S. history.

Since erupting on Tuesday, the blazes have spread among tens of thousands of acres and either damaged or destroyed more than 10,000 structures including multimillion-dollar homes.

It's still too early to assess the eventual financial cost, but a preliminary estimate by AccuWeather indicated on Thursday that the damage and economic toll was in the range of $135 billion to $150 billion. By comparison, the weather forecaster estimated that Hurricane Helen last fall resulted in losses of $225 billion to $250 billion.

“These fast-moving, wind-driven infernos have created one of the costliest wildfire disasters in modern U.S. history,” AccuWeather Chief Meteorologist Jonathan Porter said in a statement. “To put this into perspective, the total damage and economic loss from this wildfire disaster could reach nearly 4 percent of the annual GDP of the state of California.”

Insurer Exposure

Shares of insurance companies with exposure to the Los Angeles homeowners’ market on Friday sold off as investors discounted the carriers for potential payouts.

Allstate (NYSE:ALL) fell 5.6% to hit a four-month low. Chubb (CB) declined 3.4% and Travelers Cos. (TRV) slumped 4.3%. American International Group (AIG) slipped by 1.3% and Progressive (PGR) fell 1.4%. The Standard & Poor’s 500 stock index declined 1.5% for the day.

Financial-services firm J.P. Morgan estimated that Allstate (NYSE:ALL), Chubb (CB) and Travelers (TRV) are the most exposed to insured losses from the fires. Chubb (CB) may have a high exposure to high-net-worth properties, according to the firm.

“We estimate that insured losses from the event could exceed $20 billion (and even more if the fires are not controlled),” Jimmy Bhullar, analyst at J.P. Morgan, said in a January 9 report. “This would make this event significantly more severe than the 2018 Butte County Camp fires, the highest insured loss wildfires in California’s history previously (with insured losses of roughly $10 billion).”

Cincinnati Financial (CINF), Hiscox (OTCPK:HCXLF) (OTCPK:HCXLY) through Lloyds, Tokio Marine’s (OTCPK:TKOMY) (OTCPK:TKOMF) Pure and W.R. Berkley (WRB) also may be exposed to high-net-worth households in the Los Angeles region, according to Bank of America.

“The broader California market is not necessarily representative of exposures to the high-value Los Angeles residential real estate where individual home values in excess of $10 million are not uncommon (and also come with fine art, expensive jewelry, luxury cars, etc.),” Joshua Shanker, analyst at Bank of America, said in a November 8 report.

Fires Partly Contained

A key difficulty in estimating the eventual cost of the disaster is that some of the wildfires are only partly contained and may spread depending on weather conditions.

The Pacific Palisades fire was 11% contained by Sunday morning after spreading to more than 23,000 acres. The blaze is threatening wealthy neighborhoods such as Brentwood and is burning along the edge of Mandeville Canyon.

The Eaton fire in the Altadena and Pasadena area spread to more than 14,000 acres and was 15% contained by early Sunday, state fire authority Cal Fire said.

The Lidia fire in the Angeles National Forest north of Los Angeles was 100% contained, and the Hurst fire on the northern edge of the San Fernando Valley was 76% contained early Sunday. The Kenneth fire near West Hills in the western San Fernando Valley was 90% contained, authorities said.

data.title: Earnings week ahead: JPM, BAC, C, GS, TSM, UNH and more
data.description:
With earnings season kicking off in mid-January, investors are gearing up for a week of crucial reports. Leading the charge are several major banks, including Bank of America (BAC), JPMorgan Chase (JPM), Citigroup (C), Wells Fargo (WFC) and U.S. Bancorp (USB), which are set to release their results, offering valuable insights into the health of the banking sector.

Additionally, investment giants like Goldman Sachs (GS), Morgan Stanley (MS), BlackRock (BLK), Truist Financial (TFC) and PNC Financial (PNC) will provide key updates on capital markets and wealth management trends.

Beyond the financial sector, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSM), a critical player in the global semiconductor supply chain, will be closely watched. Furthermore, UnitedHealth Group (UNH), a bellwether for the healthcare sector, and Schlumberger (SLB), a leader in oilfield services, will offer valuable perspectives on their respective industries.

Below is a rundown of major quarterly updates anticipated in the week of January 13 to January 17:

Monday, January 13

KB Home (KBH)

KB Home (KBH) is scheduled to report its Q4 earnings after the market closes on Monday. Analysts expect an 18.5% year-over-year increase in EPS and nearly a 32% rise in revenue for the homebuilder.

The stock has a cautious outlook from analysts, with both sell-side analysts and Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating system assigning it a Hold rating.

Even in this uncertain atmosphere, Seeking Alpha contributor Caffital Research sees reasons for optimism. The analyst points out that the Federal Reserve's interest rate cuts have improved the housing market outlook, positioning KB Home for growth in 2025 amid an industry recovery. The structural undersupply of housing is also expected to benefit KB Home and other homebuilders, Caffital Research said, estimating a 39% upside for KBH stock.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $2.44
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $1.98B
  • Earnings Insight: KB Home has exceeded EPS expectations in 6 of the past 8 quarters, exceeding revenue in 7 of those reports.

Also reporting: ServiceTitan (TTAN), Aehr Test Systems (AEHR), Unity Bancorp (UNTY), BARK (BARK), Educational Development (EDUC), EpicQuest Education Group International Limited (EEIQ), Trio Petroleum (TPET), Vision Marine Technologies (VMAR) and more.

Tuesday, January 14

Applied Digital (APLD)

Applied Digital (APLD) is set to report its FQ2 financial results after Tuesday’s closing bell, with analysts projecting a nearly 50% decline in profits despite a ~45% increase in revenue.

Both Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system have a bullish sentiment on the stock, assigning it a Strong Buy rating.

SA contributor Hunting Alpha highlights Applied Digital’s strategic shift from operating data centers for crypto mining customers to building and managing data centers for large hyperscaler companies, positioning it for significant growth. Applied Digital has also begun outperforming revenue and EBIT expectations, the analyst pointed out, while recent fundraising efforts have bolstered its balance sheet, signaling strong investor confidence.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: -$0.15
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $61.61M
  • Earnings Insight: APLD has exceeded EPS expectations in only 1 of the past 8 quarters, exceeding revenue in 5 of those reports.

Also reporting: Karooooo (KARO), Calavo Growers (CVGW), FingerMotion (FNGR), Rocky Mountain Chocolate Factory (RMCF), Jewett-Cameron Trading (JCTC), NextTrip (NTRP), Pineapple Financial (PAPL) and more.

Wednesday, January 15

Wednesday is shaping up to be a busy day for the financial sector, with several major players set to release their quarterly results. Watch for updates from Citigroup (C), Wells Fargo (WFC), JPMorgan (JPM), Goldman Sachs (GS) and BlackRock (BLK).

Here’s a preview of the key information ahead of these reports:

Also reporting: Kinder Morgan (KMI), The Bank of New York Mellon Corporation (BK), Synovus Financial (SNV), Home Bancshares (Conway, AR) (HOMB), Texas Capital Bancshares (TCBI), H.B. Fuller Company (FUL), Concentrix Corporation (CNXC), NVE Corporation (NVEC), Park Aerospace (PKE), Sono-Tek Corporation (SOTK) and more.

Thursday, January 16

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM)

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) is set to report its Q4 earnings on Thursday. The company recently announced December 2024 revenue that surged 57.8% Y/Y to approximately NT$278.16B, driven by rising demand for AI applications. Q4 revenue grew 39% Y/Y to NT$868.46B, exceeding analysts' consensus estimate of NT$854.7B, according to Bloomberg.

TSMC has also made strategic strides internationally, starting mass production at its first chip manufacturing plant in Japan and planning a second facility for 6-12 nanometer logic chips. Reports suggest the company is in talks to build a third plant in Japan to produce advanced 3-nanometer chips.

SA author Hataf Capital highlights TSMC as a compelling investment due to its dominance in advanced semiconductor manufacturing, leadership in cutting-edge nodes (3nm, 5nm, 7nm), and strategic expansions driving growth and margin improvements. Despite geopolitical risks and market cyclicality, TSMC’s strategic initiatives and diversified revenue streams effectively mitigate these concerns, the analyst stated, justifying its Strong Buy rating.

Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating system maintain a Strong Buy recommendation on the stock.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $2.22
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $25.99B
  • Earnings Insight: TSMC has beaten EPS expectations in 7 of the past 8 quarters, missing revenue estimates 5 times in that span.

Bank of America (BAC)

Bank of America (BAC) is scheduled to release its earnings before the market opens on Thursday, alongside Morgan Stanley (MS). The announcement follows a day after earnings reports from JPMorgan (JPM), Citigroup (C), BlackRock (BLK) and Wells Fargo (WFC). Analysts forecast EPS growth of over 100% year-over-year for Bank of America's Q4 results.

Recently, both HSBC and UBS upgraded Bank of America to a Buy rating, citing the benefits of a higher-for-longer interest rate environment for the money center bank.

SA contributor On the Pulse predicts a strong 2025 for Bank of America, driven by the Federal Reserve's higher-for-longer rate stance, which boosts net interest income. The U.S. economy's growth and record stock market highs create a favorable setting for cyclical bank stocks, including Bank of America.

Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating system maintains a cautious stance on the stock, assigning a Hold rating. Meanwhile, Wall Street analysts express bullish sentiment, with a consensus Buy rating for Bank of America.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $0.77
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $25.10B
  • Earnings Insight: The bank has exceeded EPS expectations in 6 of the past 8 quarters, beating revenue estimates in 7 of those reports.·

Also reporting: UnitedHealth Group (UNH), Morgan Stanley (MS), Infosys Limited (INFY), The PNC Financial Services Group (PNC), U.S. Bancorp (USB), M&T Bank Corporation (MTB), J.B. Hunt Transport Services (JBHT), First Horizon Corporation (FHN), Pinnacle Financial Partners (PNFP), Bank OZK (OZK), WaFd (WAFD), Polestar Automotive Holding UK PLC (PSNY), Westamerica Bancorporation (WABC), Gatos Silver (GATO), Insteel Industries (IIIN), Bank7 (BSVN), VOXX International (VOXX), Canterbury Park Holding (CPHC), Anixa Biosciences (ANIX), RF Industries (RFIL), New Horizon Aircraft (HOVR) and more.

Friday, January 17

Schlumberger Limited (SLB)

Schlumberger (SLB), the world’s largest oilfield service company, is set to report its Q4 earnings before the market opens on Friday. Following mixed Q3 results, the company reaffirmed its outlook for full-year adjusted EBITDA margins of 25% or higher, supported in part by cost-cutting measures.

Wall Street analysts maintain a Buy rating on SLB, while Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating system adopts a more cautious stance with a Hold rating.

SA author Rob Barnett highlights Schlumberger's sensitivity to oil prices, noting that 80% of its revenues are influenced by oil prices 15 months prior, complicating valuation. However, despite being asset-intensive, the analyst argues that SLB is currently undervalued relative to its intrinsic value, presenting potential upside if oil prices recover.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $0.90
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $9.19B
  • Earnings Insight: The company has beaten EPS estimates in 8 straight quarters and revenue estimates in 5 of those reports.

Also reporting: Truist Financial Corporation (TFC), Fastenal Company (FAST), Wipro Limited (WIT), State Street Corporation (STT), Regions Financial Corporation (RF), Citizens Financial Group (CFG), Webster Financial Corporation (WBS), Independent Bank (INDB) and more.

data.title: Biden releases partial guidance on clean fuel subsidies, leaves final rules to Trump
data.description:
The Biden administration on Friday released short-term guidance on how companies can secure Section 45Z Clean Fuels production tax credits under the Inflation Reduction Act but did not finalize the program's key details, allowing the incoming Trump presidency to either back or reject the plan.

The new guidance details that eligible fuel must be used for transportation - such as fuels used in highway vehicles or aircraft - and says the Treasury Department plans to detail rules on how climate-smart agriculture practices for producing clean fuels can claim the credits, although the rules may not be proposed before Trump takes office.

Biofuels groups expressed disappointment in the proposal's lack of key details, and the move puts many biofuel companies in limbo, as Trump has pledged to repeal Biden's 2022 Inflation Reduction Act, which launched the program, but would need congressional support ro do so.

Biofuels firms are eager for clarity on the tax credits for fuels that combat climate change, which they hope will ultimately provide a pathway for corn-based ethanol to expand its market as a feedstock for sustainable aviation fuel.

Regarding used cooking oil as a feedstock, the Treasury proposal declined to include it in the 45Z credit and declared it ineligible until further guidance is provided, a win for U.S. farmers, who have been counting on a boom in soy-heavy biofuels such as renewable diesel to sell their crops.

Soybean oil futures for March jumped by the exchange limit on the CBOT Friday, surging 7%, the most since June 2023, shares of Bunge (NYSE:BG), the world's biggest oilseed processor, jumped 4.9%, while Darling Ingredients (DAR) and Valero Energy (NYSE:VLO), the joint owners of Diamond Green Diesel, North America's biggest producer of renewable diesel maker, gained 7.8% and 1.5% respectively.

Other potentially relevant stocks include Bloom Energy (BE), Clean Energy Fuels (CLNE), Gevo (GEVO), Green Plains (GPRE), REX American Resources (REX).

ETFs: (ICLN), (QCLN), (PBW), (PBD), (ACES), (CNRG), (ERTH), (SMOG)

data.title: Zuckerberg slams Apple for lack of invention, 'squeezing people' for money
data.description:
Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) top boss Mark Zuckerberg on Friday criticized tech behemoth Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) in an interview with Joe Rogan, host of one of the world's most popular podcasts. The Facebook-founder called the iPhone-maker no longer innovative, and said the company was "squeezing people" to make money.

In a nearly three-hour talk that ranged through various topics of discussion, Zuckerberg touched upon Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) and its practices. Rogan has 19M subscribers on YouTube, and his "Joe Rogan Experience" podcast has reached billions of viewers over its existence.

"They haven't really invented anything great in a while. It's like Steve Jobs invented the iPhone, and now they're just kind of sitting on it 20 years later," the Meta (NASDAQ:META) chief executive said. He added that Apple's (NASDAQ:AAPL) annual iPhone sales have become generally "flat to declining."

See the following chart for Apple's (NASDAQ:AAPL) iPhone revenue numbers this decade:

"So how are they making more money as a company? Well, they do it by basically squeezing people, and...having this 30% tax on developers. By getting you to buy more peripherals and things that plug into it," Zuckerberg told Rogan, referring to the commission Apple (AAPL) charges for transactions made through its App Store and in-app payment system.

Hurting Competition

Zuckerberg commented on Apple's (AAPL) strategy of creating an ecosystem for consumers wherein the most optimum performance will be achieved only through AAPL's products.

"They build stuff like AirPods, which are cool, but they've just thoroughly hamstrung the ability for anyone else to build something that can connect to the iPhone in the same way," the Meta (NASDAQ:META) CEO said.

"Apple (AAPL) has a specific protocol that they've built into the iPhone that allows AirPods to basically connect to it, and it's just much more seamless because they've enabled that, but they don't let anyone else use the protocols. If they did, they would probably be much better competitors to AirPods out there," he said.

"And whenever you push on this thing, they get super touchy, and they basically wrap their defense of it in 'well, if we let other companies plug into our thing, then that would violate people's privacy and security,'" Zuckerberg added.

'Twice as Much Profit'

The creator of Facebook believes that if Apple (AAPL) were to open up its products to make them more easily accessible to other competitors, Meta (META) alone could stand to make double its profit.

"At some point, I did this back of the envelope calculation of all the random rules that Apple (AAPL) puts out. If they didn't apply, and this is just Meta (META), I think we'd like make twice as much profit or something, and that's just us," Zuckerberg said.

In its most recently reported quarter, Meta (META) reaped $15.69B in net income, a jump of 35% Y/Y.

The META top boss brought up an example where the company had asked Apple (AAPL) to use the same connection protocol as the AirPods for Meta's (META) Ray-Ban smart glasses

"One of the protocols that they've used, that they've built, they basically didn't encrypt it, so it's like plain text, and then they're like 'well, we can't have you plug into it because it would be insecure,'" Zuckerberg said.

"It's insecure because you didn't build any security into it and then now you're using that as a justification for why only your product can connect in an easy way," he added.

META Outperformed AAPL in 2024

Meta Platforms (META) stock surged +65.4% last year, more than double that of Apple's (AAPL) +30.1%.

The social media giant impressed investors with its quarterly performances which showed a rise in ad impressions and average price per ad. Its large language model Llama also gained solid market share.

Conversely, Apple's (AAPL) gains were capped by lingering concerns over China iPhone performance throughout last year. The firm also jumped onto the AI train in a big way with the introduction of Apple Intelligence.

data.title: Meta CEO says White House berated staff to remove posts, Trump will be good for tech
data.description:
Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) Chief Executive Mark Zuckerberg accused White House officials of berating Facebook employees while demanding that certain content be removed from the platform and said President-elect Trump will be good for the tech sector.

Zuckerberg said in an interview with Joe Rogan on the comedian’s podcast released Friday, “Basically, these people from the Biden administration would call up our team and, like, scream at them and curse. It just got to the point where we were like, ‘No, we’re not gonna, we’re not gonna take down things that are true. That’s ridiculous.’

He said that at times Facebook agreed to remove content, but added that it would act differently in the future. Facebook “made some choices that with the benefit of hindsight and new information, we wouldn’t make today.”

As an example, Zuckerberg said the White House asked for the removal of a meme showing actor Leonardo DiCaprio pointing at a TV image of an advertisement for a class-action lawsuit for those who once took a COVID-19 vaccine.

He said the Biden administration said Facebook had to remove it. “We said, ‘No, we’re not gonna, we’re not to take down humor and satire. We’re not gonna take down things that are, that are true.’”

The White House did not immediately respond to a request for comment by Seeking Alpha on Saturday.

Zuckerberg said earlier in the week that Meta would end its third-party fact-checking program in favor of a “Community Notes” program to avoid frequent and unnecessary censorship on posts across its platforms.

President Joe Biden on Friday criticized that move in a press conference held before Zuckerberg’s interview with Rogan was released.

"It’s just completely contrary to everything America is about. We want to tell the truth. We haven’t always done it as a nation," Biden said.

Meanwhile, Zuckerberg said that the tech industry is “a bright spot for the American economy,” and was optimistic that the next Trump administration would highlight its “strategic advantage” in the industry.

“I think he [President-elect Donald Trump] just wants America to win,” Zuckerberg said of Trump.

He said the US government should defend America’s tech industry overseas and that he “optimistic” Trump will do that.

Last week, Meta announced that Joel Kaplan, a well-known Republican and former deputy chief of staff to President George W. Bush, would take over as the company’s head of global policy.

Meta last month donated $1M to Trump’s inaugural fund.
